growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow now has hard evidence of AI monetization, with its April 6 SEC filing saying Now Assist exceeded $600 million of ACV in 2025 and is on track to top $1 billion in 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow has primary-source proof that AI is monetizing fast, and the stock is back near 52-week lows ahead of April 22 earnings and the May 5-7 Knowledge event, creating a favorable setup despite M&A and valuation risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Lululemon is deeply hated after a brutal rerating, yet the business still has strong international growth, meaningful buybacks, and a balance sheet that gives it time to recover.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lululemon is a beaten-down premium brand trading near 12x earnings with strong gross margins, net cash, buybacks, and double-digit international growth, but the recovery still depends on North America stabilizing and tariff pressure proving manageable.

Microsoft is a high-quality AI and cloud compounder trading about 34% below its 52-week high, with April 29 earnings and May 1 Agent 365/Frontier Suite availability as near-term catalysts, but record AI capex and regulatory scrutiny cap upside certainty.
FDA approval of AVLAYAH de-risks the story and validates Denali's BBB platform, but commercialization, confirmatory-trial, and cash-burn risks keep this from higher conviction.

Recent FDA approval for acquired hypothalamic obesity and a follow-on positive CHMP opinion de-risk near-term IMCIVREE growth, but the March EMANATE miss and ongoing cash burn keep Rhythm in the prove-it bucket rather than top-conviction biotech.

Qualys is a high-margin, cash-generative cybersecurity platform trading near its 52-week low at a modest multiple, and May 2026 earnings plus recent AI and partner-led product launches create rerating potential if management can sustain growth.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NTNX is still about 53% below its 52-week high while remaining profitable, and the setup has a real near-term rerating window into its May 27, 2026 earnings report after February's AMD strategic investment and completed $300 million ASR, though another soft cloud/AI spending guide would keep it trapped.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nutanix looks like an oversold but fundamentally solid infrastructure software name with positive free cash flow, a fresh buyback signal, and multiple dated catalysts into late May 2026 earnings, though execution slippage keeps it below strong-buy quality.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: FOUR is still about 61% below its 52-week high while remaining profitable, and Apr. 28, 2026 earnings are the near-term catalyst after Jared Isaacman bought stock twice in March, but leverage and acquisition integration risk mean a bad print can still break the setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Oversold overlap fintech with a founder open-market buy, cleaner governance, and fresh acquisition synergies ahead of the late-April earnings catalyst, but leverage and integration risk keep this below top-conviction status.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PayPal still throws off billions in cash and buybacks, but the market is pricing it like a structurally broken asset after the February reset.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap cash-generative franchise with a May 5 earnings reset under a new CEO, but the rerating depends on branded checkout stabilizing after a weak Q4 and soft 2026 guide.
ACN is a high-quality cash compounder near its 52-week low, and record Q2 bookings plus raised FY26 free-cash-flow guidance support a rerating if federal headwinds stay contained.

Kinsale remains a high-quality excess-and-surplus insurer with elite underwriting and fresh capital-return support, but softer commercial-property conditions and only modest upside into late-April earnings keep the setup short of a BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista's March 12 launch of the XPO liquid-cooled optics ecosystem gives it a cleaner path into the next density jump in AI networking, with a 12.8 Tbps module and 4x rack-unit throughput improvement aimed directly at scale-up and scale-out GPU fabrics.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Elite AI-networking execution and strong cash generation keep Arista attractive, but a still-rich multiple and heavy hyperscaler concentration limit upside at current levels.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Eaton is monetizing AI power scarcity with a January 28 modular data-center launch that cuts deployment schedules by about 35% on average and a February earnings release showing Electrical Americas orders accelerating on data-center momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ETN is a real AI-power and electrification winner with record backlog and accelerating data-center orders, but near $391 the stock already discounts much of that strength.

catalysts_general: Discovery: IperionX posted a four-insider open-market buying cluster tied to March 27, 2026 trades: CEO Anastasios Arima bought about $1.33 million, Executive Chair Todd Hannigan about $498.2K, President Toby Symonds about $262.6K, and director R. Tony Tripeny about $99.5K, or roughly $2.19 million combined.
growth_picks.analyst_take: U.S. titanium reshoring support and a rare insider-buying cluster make IPX strategically interesting, but it remains a pre-profit scale-up story with heavy cash burn and execution risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CoStar qualifies as a high-signal insider setup because the CEO stepped in with a $2.48M buy after a steep drawdown and at least one more executive buy followed, creating a real cluster rather than a token gesture.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality real estate data franchise with real April earnings and buyback catalysts, but the stock still needs to prove Homes.com monetization and margin expansion before it deserves a more aggressive rating.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: AVOID candidate: Duolingo's February guidance openly prioritized free-user growth and teaching improvements over near-term monetization just as AI makes basic tutoring, translation, and conversational practice cheaper, which raises the risk that engagement stays high while pricing power fades.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Duolingo remains a high-quality, cash-generative education platform, but management's 2026 decision to sacrifice near-term monetization for user growth and the low-switching-cost AI threat cap upside until Q1 execution proves the reset is working.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Even without a full closure of Hormuz, tanker dislocation is keeping spot rates higher for longer, which favors spot-exposed crude tanker owners like International Seaways more than the market appears to be discounting.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Strong Q1 tanker-rate exposure and a clean balance sheet support earnings, but INSW is trading near its 52-week high with limited valuation cushion if geopolitically driven spot rates normalize.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Symbotic looks like a credible physical-AI second derivative because on March 31 it won a major new AWG warehouse automation project expected to process nearly 19 million cases annually, reinforcing that AI-enabled robotics is still landing large-scale supply-chain deployments.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Symbotic has real growth momentum and fresh customer wins, but the stock still looks too dependent on Walmart and too expensive to buy aggressively here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: IP popped up because CEO Andrew Silvernail made a rare roughly $2.0M open-market buy into a restructuring story, which is exactly the kind of insider signal worth respecting in a hated cyclical.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CEO insider buying and a planned EMEA spin-off create a plausible re-rating path, but soft corrugated demand and DS Smith integration risk still make International Paper a prove-it turnaround.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The Administration is explicitly tying LNG policy to disruption risk from Iran and its proxies, and Venture Global has a live near-term volume catalyst because Plaquemines received a March 13, 2026 immediate export uplift while CP2 continues to advance through federal export approvals.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Venture Global has real LNG volume and contract catalysts, but extreme leverage, huge negative free cash flow, ongoing customer disputes, and no clear valuation discount keep it out of buy territory.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Baxter is about 47% below its 52-week high and only modestly off its lows, with April 30, 2026 earnings as the check-in on the medical-products turnaround and balance-sheet repair, but the bear case is that product, litigation, and leverage issues can keep the rerating delayed.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Baxter screens cheap and is de-levering, but flat 2026 guidance and unresolved Novum IQ infusion-pump issues keep the turnaround too fragile to underwrite as a buy yet.

catalysts_general: Discovery: On April 8, 2026 Benzinga flagged a bullish call sweep in APLD's April 10 $30 calls, with 323 contracts split across 28 trades and the full strike trading 12,845 contracts against 8,351 open interest, just as the company reported fiscal Q3 revenue of $126.6 million and kept adding credit support around CoreWeave-linked leases.
growth_picks.analyst_take: APLD is proving demand for its AI data-center platform with a strong Q3 revenue/EBITDA inflection, but the capital structure, CoreWeave concentration, and insider-selling tape keep the setup too fragile for a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Novavax is back on the radar because the activism campaign escalated again on April 8, creating a live strategic-review and proxy-fight angle in a stock with a small enough float and valuation to move violently on change.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NVAX has credible strategic optionality from the April 8 activist escalation and Matrix-M monetization deals with Pfizer and Sanofi, but the 2026 revenue reset and partner dependence keep it below buyable quality.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: AVOID - Workday's weak February outlook and its fresh April 8 stock drop after Anthropic launched Managed Agents both reinforce how vulnerable seat-based HR and finance SaaS can look when agentic AI starts doing workflow work directly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality HR and finance software franchise with strong cash flow and AI optionality, but the FY27 growth reset and rising agentic-AI disruption risk keep the stock in prove-it mode.

Eli Lilly still has elite obesity and diabetes growth plus near-term pipeline catalysts, but at roughly 41x trailing earnings and only 16.5% below its 52-week high, the setup looks high quality rather than mispriced.

Rambus has real AI-memory and DDR5/HBM4 exposure with strong margins and an April 27 earnings catalyst, but the stock is still priced richly relative to its growth while customer concentration, a CFO transition, and insider selling limit upside.
Coty is statistically cheap and has real balance-sheet repair plus strategic-review optionality, but withdrawn FY26 guidance and a sharply weaker Q3 outlook keep it as a wait-for-proof turnaround.

EchoStar's March deleveraging steps are real, but the stock is already trading near its 52-week high while revenue, free cash flow and liquidity still look too weak to justify chasing an event-driven rerating.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Whirlpool is down about 50% from its 52-week high and still sits close to the lows despite remaining profitable, with April 23, 2026 earnings as the catalyst for any margin or cash-flow stabilization; the bear case is that housing turnover and consumer-ticket demand stay weak for another quarter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Whirlpool is optically cheap, but leverage, dilution, and housing-linked demand weakness outweigh the late-April earnings catalyst and March insider buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Plug is still speculative, but it now has the kind of hated-turning setup that matters because gross margin finally flipped positive and management laid out a clearer path to EBITDA breakeven in late 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Plug Power has credible 2026 liquidity and backlog catalysts, but the business still burns too much cash and depends on asset monetization and capital access before profitability is proven.
